title: Run Your Stall
developmental_frame: In the capstone, Wealth is the live stall on Showcase Day. At 9–10 a child can set simple prices, offer to real visitors (Q1), keep a tally (Q3), and then save/spend/share what they earn (Q2) — the whole year's money and value skills in one real event. This is also the final project of the year, so it closes with a reflection on how far they've come.
authentic_value_meaning: Value here is running a real little stall, earning a little real money, and deciding what to do with it. It's every wealth skill from the year — make, offer, price, tally, manage — brought to life in front of real customers, for real.

motivation_lever.mechanism: The child runs a genuine stall with real customers and real (small) money on a real day. The stakes are exciting and safe: actual guests deciding to buy, an actual tally climbing, actual earnings to manage afterward. Nothing motivates the year's wealth lessons like a real till at the end of a real event.

artifact: A stall run on Showcase Day with a tally of sales, followed by a save/spend/share plan for the earnings — plus a short reflection on the whole year.
success_criteria: The child set simple prices and ran a stall for real guests.
They kept a tally of what sold and what they earned.
They made a save/spend/share plan for the earnings and can reflect on their year.

lessons.title: Set your prices and offers
lessons.concept: Decide what you'll sell and for how much, using your little-business skills. Keep prices simple and fair — a price is you saying what your thing is worth. Have your digital sign show the prices clearly.
lessons.real_example: 'Bookmarks RM2, or 3 for RM5.' Simple, clear, and the '3 for RM5' gives people a reason to buy more. Fair prices that a guest can understand in a second.
lessons.apply_task: Set a simple price for each thing you're selling. Write them on your sign.
lessons.reflection_prompt: Are your prices fair — not too cheap, not too dear? Think back to what you learned about pricing this year.
lessons.feedback_task: Show your prices to a parent and check they feel fair for what you made.
lessons.teach_back_task: Explain to someone why a clear, fair price helps a stall sell.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: Your prices depend on your effort and your real guests — you decide them. A computer's guess matters less than what feels fair to you.
success_criteria: Simple, fair prices are set and shown on the sign.

lessons.title: Run the stall
lessons.concept: On the day, run your stall: greet guests warmly, tell them about your things, and make your offers. Use your listening skill — a friendly stall-holder who listens sells more than a pushy one.
lessons.real_example: 'Hi! These are bookmarks I made — this sparkly one's my favourite. Would you like one?' Friendly, honest, and you listen to what they say. That's how a real stall feels.
lessons.apply_task: Run your stall on Showcase Day. Greet each guest, offer your things kindly, and make some real sales.
lessons.reflection_prompt: How did it feel to sell to real guests? Easier or harder than you expected?
lessons.feedback_task: Notice which of your offers guests responded to best. That's real-time learning.
lessons.teach_back_task: Tell someone the story of a sale you made and how you made it.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: Running a real stall with real guests is fully human — warmth, listening, offering. No screen does this for you; it's your moment.
success_criteria: The stall was run for real guests with real offers made.

lessons.title: Keep the tally
lessons.concept: As you sell, keep your tally — what sold, and what you earned. At the end, add it up. Your tally tells the true story of the day: what people wanted and how you did.
lessons.real_example: End-of-day tally: 5 bookmarks sold, RM11 earned, and the sparkly ones went first. Real numbers that show you exactly what worked.
lessons.apply_task: Keep your tally through the stall, then add up your total earnings at the end.
lessons.reflection_prompt: What does your tally show about what guests liked most? Same lesson as your little business — the tally teaches.
lessons.feedback_task: Show your final tally to a parent and talk about what sold best and why.
lessons.teach_back_task: Show someone your tally and what it tells you about the day.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: Keeping and adding your own tally helps you understand your own day — do the counting yourself.
success_criteria: A tally of sales and total earnings was kept and totted up.

lessons.title: Save, spend, share — and look back
lessons.concept: Now use your three-jar skill on real earnings: decide how much to save, spend, and share, on purpose. Then look back on your whole year — everything you learned to get to this day. That's a lot to be proud of.
lessons.real_example: You earned RM11: maybe RM5 to save, RM4 to spend, RM2 to share. And you look back — a year ago you couldn't finish a project; today you planned and ran a whole showcase. That's real growth.
lessons.apply_task: Split your earnings into save/spend/share on purpose. Then write or tell one thing you're proudest of learning this year.
lessons.reflection_prompt: Looking across the whole year — noticing, making, telling stories, running a stall — what changed most about what you can do?
lessons.feedback_task: Sit with a parent and share your save/spend/share plan and your proudest moment of the year.
lessons.teach_back_task: Tell a family member the story of your whole year of learning, ending with Showcase Day.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: How you split real earnings and what you're proud of are deeply yours. This is your year — own it, in your own words.
success_criteria: Earnings were split across save/spend/share on purpose.
The child reflected on their growth across the whole year.
